items.blurb: Torn-paper streetscapes in muted tones — one strip and your journal page suddenly has a quiet little neighborhood of its own.
The workhorse of the collage drawer: soft scraps of old-paper texture that make every layout look effortlessly curated.
Moody, ink-washed tones that fade like the last ten minutes of daylight. Journaling at golden hour, bottled.
Stars you can see at noon — faded constellations scattered over paper scraps, ready to drift across your spreads.
Sunny citrus tones with that worn-paper softness, like a summer afternoon pressed flat and rolled up for later.
Warm, windswept tones torn straight from an October afternoon — the fastest way to give a page that crisp-air feeling.
Soft colour fragments that somehow always agree with each other. Layer it anywhere and the page just works.
Faded ticket stubs from journeys that never happened — instant nostalgia for your planner, no travel required.
Made for the margins: gentle paper-scrap collage that frames your handwriting like it was always meant to be there.
Mossy greens and leafy scraps that turn a plain page into a quiet walk under the trees.
Watercolour skies torn into soft strips — for the days you want your journal to look up instead of down.
Every good green in one roll, from sage to deep leaf. The plant shelf of washi tapes.
Little repeating shapes with an easy beat to them — collage that hums along while you write.
Postmarks, scraps and traces of letters passed between friends — correspondence energy for every envelope you seal.
Blossoms dissolving into soft mist — springtime in tape form, and dangerously easy to use on everything.
Spare little shapes and paper textures in perfect balance — the minimalist's gateway into collage.
Stackable shapes in gentle colours, like a toy box drawn from memory. Playful without ever getting loud.
Old-book elegance in a roll: engraved flourishes and aged paper for spreads with a library hush.
A curated mix of Yohaku's favourite fragments — a greatest-hits roll for when you can't pick just one mood.
Deep-sky fragments and quiet constellations — stick a strip down and dim the lights on your page.
Ledger lines, stamps and official-looking scraps gone soft and lovely — paperwork you'll actually enjoy.
Hazy moonlit scraps that feel like a dream remembered at breakfast — soft, silvery, and quietly magic.
Wind-flowers in faded ink, scattered along the roll like petals someone pressed into a favourite book.
Gentle rain rendered in soft blues and paper texture — cozy-day energy for every strip you tear.
June hydrangeas in dusty blues and purples — rainy-season romance for letters and layouts alike.
Mid-century scraps and faded prints with just the right amount of yesteryear — nostalgia by the metre.
Deep greens and shadowy branches — the mysterious, moonlit cousin of your favourite botanical tape.
A hand-tied bundle of blooms running down the roll — flowers for your pages that never wilt.
Aged labels, worn edges and time-softened colours — flea-market treasure that dispenses by the strip.
Fragments that sway in three-four time — graceful little scraps that waltz across any layout.
Soft-focus sea creatures drifting through paper-blue water — a tiny aquarium visit every time you journal.
Stamps, postmarks and far-away greetings on one dreamy roll — made for people who still believe in mail.
Card catalogues, due-date slips and quiet shelves — bookish charm for margins and mail alike.
A wandering cat pauses under the cherry blossoms — a springtime scene worth mailing, or framing and keeping all to yourself.
One brave little cat under a glittering winter sky — this starry postcard makes even a quick hello feel like a snow-globe moment.
Notes to self, served by a distinguished tuxedo cat who runs his own make-believe cafe. Thirty sheets of the coziest reminders you'll ever write.
A solid brass page clip, hand-finished in Okayama and stamped with artist eric's tiny art — the kind of desk object that ages into an heirloom.
Event-only stickers from artist eric, packed with hand-stamped charm — a small souvenir from a collab you'll wish you'd attended.
192 blank pages wrapped in eric's gentle illustrations, plus an illustrated guide board to keep your lines honest. A journal to fill slowly.
A notebook made entirely of rescued washi paper and dressed in an eric design — sustainability that happens to be adorable.
A tiny brass astronaut clutching a fountain pen, hand-cast in Okayama — proof that stationery love reaches all the way to orbit.
Tear off a Moomin and a moon phase every morning — a soft grey-and-yellow daily ritual sized for the smallest desk corner.
A fresh eric stamp illustration for every single day of 2026 — 365 small reasons to look forward to tomorrow.
eric's tiny colour illustrations, one square day at a time — a mini calendar that earns its desk space through sheer charm.
Part calendar, part notebook, all eric — a little illustration smiles from each month's corner, and a pen comes along to seal the deal.
A gentle bear offering a single bloom — stamp it wherever a page needs a small act of kindness.
A bear sunk deep into couch mode — the official stamp of well-earned lazy evenings and rest-day journal entries.
A bear settling in for coffee and cake — stamp your planner and suddenly every afternoon is a cafe date.
One delighted bear, arms up, thoroughly amazed. For journal days that genuinely deserve a wow.
A bear and its donut, together at last — the sweetest way to mark snack breaks and small victories.
A bear mid-flight, defying physics with total confidence — stamp it on days when anything feels possible.
A patient little bear, waiting politely — perfect for to-do lists, countdowns, and packages that can't arrive soon enough.
A studious bear hard at work with pen in paw — the ideal mascot for journaling sessions and letters home.
A bear demonstrating expert-level relaxation — stamp it as a gentle reminder that breaks are productive too.
A bear sipping happily through a straw — instant refreshment for planner pages and hydration trackers.
A bear doing its very best cat impression, nyao and all — dreamlike silliness in stampable form.
A bear pausing for a proper cup of tea — steep, stamp, and let your journal exhale.
A bear cradling a fresh loaf like treasure — bakery-core joy for planners, mail, and everything between.
A hand-carved muffin small enough to bake into any margin — snack-sized joy for planners and gift tags.
A black cat guarding a pile of apples, carved by hand — autumnal storybook charm in one satisfying press.
A cat soaring through the air with zero explanation — hand-carved whimsy for pages that need a little lift.
One sturdy hand-carved tree, ready to plant a tiny forest across your journal, envelopes, and gift wrap.
A generous cat arriving with a present — the perfect stamp for birthday cards and thank-you notes with personality.
A quiet girl holding wheat, carved with storybook softness — a golden-hour touch for letters and layouts.
Obi1's vintage-flavoured take on the calendar-notebook combo, with a mini illustration in every month's corner and a pen included. Retro desk goals.
A retro bear setting off on holiday, pre-inked and ready — stamp bon voyage onto every letter and luggage tag.
A friendly ghost on postal duty, drawn in Obi1's retro style — spooky-sweet stamping for Halloween mail and beyond.
A vintage sweets-shop scene, pre-inked for instant charm — like stamping a little candy counter onto your page.
Deep-sea mail delivery in nostalgic ink — because even the bottom of the ocean deserves cute correspondence.
About a hundred wax beads in navy, mint and red, tucked into Obi1's retro packaging — melt, press, and make every envelope an event.
A panda-postal wax seal head in Obi1's retro style — one press and your letters graduate from mail to keepsake.
The Amaoto Soda wax seal head — fizzy retro nostalgia cast in alloy, ready to crown your prettiest envelopes.
A washi tape scrapbook of eric's travels across the U.S. — workshop memories you can wrap around anything.
A palm-sized page-a-day calendar by eric — small enough that yesterday's page becomes today's planner sticker.
Nine chunky vinyl stickers of eric's favourite little things, born from a Houston collab — laptop and journal upgrades, sorted.
A rust-proof aluminum watercolor palette with magnetic pans, dressed in eric's artwork — serious painting gear that doubles as a keepsake.
A solid brass pin of eric's astronaut toting a tiny tote bag — hand-finished in Okayama and destined for your favourite jacket.
eric's illustrated tribute to handcraft itself, on a roll — collab keepsakes from a 2025 event, seven metres at a time.
A blush-pink calf leather A6 cover, hand-stitched in Okayama — the forever home your favourite notebook has been waiting for.
Girly tracing-paper stickers with hot-stamped shimmer that winks in the light — three dollars of pure planner sparkle.
